scoring_rubric.good: opportunity is ongoing/upcoming with enough time for local deliverable prep
AI, infra, Web3, public-good, or developer-tooling theme matches local assets
funding/prize pool and currency/token route are explicit
repo/demo/writeup can be produced locally without account or wallet
eligibility, judging, team, and open-source requirements are visible
no wallet/payment/prize gate is needed before local proof
prior BUIDLs do not already saturate the idea
does not require immediate public campaign or donor coordination
local artifact can be reused for Gitcoin, EF ESP, or DoraHacks even if not submitted

source_gate_notes: At least eight public/current or local sources are recorded.
BUIDL, hackathon, grant, bounty, wallet, payment, public action, and runtime surfaces are separated.
At least twenty opportunity fields are defined.
At least six route stages are defined.
At least eight scoring factors are defined.
At least ten hard kill reasons are defined.
Boundary proves no account, wallet, profile, BUIDL, registration, submission, vote, donation, payment, public action, worker/runtime, model/MCP call, or external side effect occurred.
DoraHacks positions itself as an open-source incentive and hackathon community platform.
The page references hackathon organizer tooling, BUIDL AI, BUIDLs, grants, premium organizer tooling, and wallet/login surfaces.
The hackathon page says participants can learn, team up, build, participate in hackathons, or create hackathons.
It exposes filters for ongoing/upcoming/closed status, virtual/IRL location, region, and tags such as AI, Quantum, Crypto/PQC, Biohack, and Student.
Creating or participating in a hackathon is not performed by this packet.
The grant page presents quadratic funding and MACI/aMACI style grant rounds.
It reports public aggregate stats on total rounds, votes, and funded amount, and exposes filters for ongoing/upcoming/closed rounds, ecosystem, and voting method.
Voting, donating, grant participation, or wallet actions are blocked here.
The BUIDL page describes DoraHacks as a home for BUIDLs and says users can create projects and accelerate ventures.
It exposes technology/category/chain filters and public aggregate counts for BUIDLs, funded amount, and active builders.
Creating a BUIDL, updating milestones, or joining a campaign is gated.
The sitemap was reachable and fresh with 2026-06-17 timestamps.
It lists routes for BUIDL, grant, bounty, faucet, search, message, BUIDL AI, hackathon, bug bounty, legal terms, cookie policy, and chain-specific BUIDL pages.
Use sitemap routes as a public discovery map, not as permission to interact.
Terms of Use were reachable and last updated 5 May 2026.
Terms identify Hackerlink Limited and affiliated companies, create a legally binding agreement, and mention arbitration and dispute provisions.
Account, organizer, submission, legal, payment, or prize participation requires human review.
Privacy Policy was reachable and last updated 26 November 2025.
It covers personal data, usage data, cookies, transfers, rights, retention, and jurisdiction-specific notices.
Any account, profile, KYC-like, contact, or project submission route must treat personal-data processing as gated.
Bug bounty page was reachable, but public text showed no ongoing bounties and seven outdated bounties at capture time.
Bug bounty work should not be conflated with grants/hackathons and should remain monitor-only unless a live scoped program appears.
The public GitHub organization is reachable and can support read-only source discovery or duplicate checks.
GitHub issues, PRs, comments, forks, and public actions remain gated.
The Gitcoin packet established grant-lane controls around fit, rules, identity, wallet, submission, public action, and payout gates.
Reuse the same no-submit local-fit posture for DoraHacks.
Prior Web3 lane refresh already separated grant, hackathon, bounty, wallet, and submission surfaces.
DoraHacks should enter the queue as a scout template before any project creation or submission.
